questions.question: What is the primary characteristic of SIMD systems?
questions.explanation: SIMD stands for Single Instruction, Multiple Data, which means the same instruction is applied to multiple data points simultaneously.

questions.question: Which type of parallel computer is characterized by multiple processing units, each executing its own instructions?
questions.explanation: MIMD stands for Multiple Instruction, Multiple Data, which means multiple processing units execute different instructions simultaneously.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an interconnection network in a parallel computer?
options: A) To connect processing units to memory
B) To connect processing units to each other
questions.explanation: Interconnection networks connect processing units to each other, enabling communication and data exchange between them.

questions.question: What is the main difference between shared-memory and distributed-memory parallel computers?
options: A) Shared-memory computers have multiple processors, while distributed-memory computers have a single processor
B) Shared-memory computers have multiple processors, while distributed-memory computers have a single processor
C) Shared-memory computers have a single processor, while distributed-memory computers have multiple processors
D) Shared-memory computers have a single processor, while distributed-memory computers have a single processor
questions.explanation: Shared-memory computers have multiple processors that share a common memory space, while distributed-memory computers have multiple processors with separate memory spaces.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of cache coherence in parallel systems?
questions.explanation: Cache coherence ensures that data is consistent across all cache levels, reducing cache thrashing and improving overall system performance.
